# clickstack_dashboard (Resource)

Manages a ClickStack dashboard.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "clickstack_dashboard" "example" {
  name = "Service Overview"
  tags = ["production", "sre"]

  tiles {
    x = 0
    y = 0
    w = 12
    h = 8

    config {
      name           = "Request Rate"
      display_type   = "line"
      source         = data.clickstack_sources.all.sources[0].id
      group_by       = "ServiceName"
      where_language = "lucene"
      granularity    = "5 minute"

      select {
        agg_fn           = "count"
        value_expression = ""
      }
    }
  }

  tiles {
    x = 12
    y = 0
    w = 12
    h = 8

    config {
      name           = "Error Count"
      display_type   = "number"
      source         = data.clickstack_sources.all.sources[0].id
      where_language = "lucene"

      select {
        agg_fn                 = "count"
        value_expression       = ""
        agg_condition          = "SeverityText:ERROR"
        agg_condition_language = "lucene"
      }
    }
  }
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `name` (String) The name of the dashboard.

### Optional

- `filters` (Block List) Dashboard-level filters. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--filters))
- `saved_filter_values` (Block List) Persistent filter defaults. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--saved_filter_values))
- `saved_query` (String) Default saved query for the dashboard.
- `saved_query_language` (String) Language for the saved query: sql or lucene.
- `tags` (List of String) Tags for the dashboard.
- `tiles` (Block List) Dashboard tiles/charts. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--tiles))

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) The unique identifier of the dashboard.

<a id="nestedblock--filters"></a>
### Nested Schema for `filters`

Required:

- `expression` (String) Column/field for filtering.
- `name` (String) Human-readable filter label.
- `source_id` (String) Associated data source.
- `type` (String) Filter type (QUERY_EXPRESSION).


<a id="nestedblock--saved_filter_values"></a>
### Nested Schema for `saved_filter_values`

Required:

- `condition` (String) SQL filter expression.

Optional:

- `type` (String) Filter type (defaults to sql).


<a id="nestedblock--tiles"></a>
### Nested Schema for `tiles`

Required:

- `h` (Number) Height in grid units.
- `w` (Number) Width in grid units.
- `x` (Number) Horizontal position.
- `y` (Number) Vertical position.

Optional:

- `config` (Block List) Tile configuration (exactly one block). (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--tiles--config))

<a id="nestedblock--tiles--config"></a>
### Nested Schema for `tiles.config`

Required:

- `display_type` (String) Chart type: line, table, number, search, or markdown.
- `name` (String) Tile display name.

Optional:

- `content` (String) Content for markdown tiles.
- `fields` (List of String) Fields for search-type displays.
- `granularity` (String) Time granularity (e.g. 5 minute, 1 hour).
- `group_by` (String) Field to group results by.
- `select` (Block List) Aggregation specifications. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--tiles--config--select))
- `sort_order` (String) Sort order: asc or desc.
- `source` (String) Data source identifier.
- `where` (String) Filter condition for the tile.
- `where_language` (String) Filter language: sql or lucene.

<a id="nestedblock--tiles--config--select"></a>
### Nested Schema for `tiles.config.select`

Required:

- `agg_fn` (String) Aggregation function: avg, count, max, min, sum, count_distinct, last_value, quantile.

Optional:

- `agg_condition` (String) Aggregation filter condition (e.g. SeverityText:ERROR).
- `agg_condition_language` (String) Language for agg_condition: sql or lucene.
- `alias` (String) Display name for the aggregation.
- `level` (Number) Percentile level for quantile aggregation (0.5-0.99).
- `value_expression` (String) Column or expression to aggregate.

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
terraform import clickstack_dashboard.example 69cafb78dacf4418bedbb174
```
